meaning: vt.指明,指出,任命,指派v.指定,指派
examples: Japan's prime minister-designate is completing his cabinet today.
There are efforts under way to designate the bridge a historic landmark.
If you want to designate a period, you have to look at tangible things like pottery, particularly painted pottery, because it's easier to categorize.
Each Member State and non-governmental organisation may designate no more than three candidates for each biennium.
And more advance directives are likely to designate friends as health and financial decisionmakers.
